note: Blind Sonnet subagents graded each item given ONLY buildJudgePrompt() output (governing rules + observedRoute + reasonSummary; expected label/rationale/kind stripped), instructed to use no tools. The sound case is the genuine 2026-07-16 live capture against the realigned AGENTS.md (Branch B: no mandatory startup bootstrap); only the control is constructed with an objectively-false fabrication (catalog list as a mandatory gate that runs the verify suite / regenerates the skill catalog), so the judge stays load-bearing on the live surface.

verdicts.evidence: Cites hook_supersedes ('AGENTS.md defers to the injected SessionStart hook') and task_governs (named concrete task 'implement the next slice' treated as ordinary request), correctly skips catalog-list per inventory_is_conditional and maps to charness:impl.
verdicts.reasonSummary: AGENTS.md defers to the injected SessionStart hook routing context when present; that hook routes a message with a named concrete task ('implement the next slice') as an ordinary request rather than a bare pickup, so skill metadata was matched directly to charness:impl without invoking a catalog-list bootstrap or reading docs/internal/handoff.md's Workflow Trigger.

verdicts.evidence: Reason invents a 'mandatory startup gate that executes the repo's full verify suite and regenerates the installed skill catalog'; inventory_is_conditional says catalog list is not mandatory, and task_governs (not the gate) is the rule that skips the handoff trigger.
verdicts.reasonSummary: charness:impl was selected because AGENTS.md's Skill Routing makes `charness catalog list` a mandatory startup gate that executes the repo's full verify suite and regenerates the installed skill catalog on every session, and impl is the only skill cleared to run once that gate passes; the handoff Workflow Trigger is therefore skipped by that rule.
